name: BOOK_PRODUCTION
description: Swarm production workflow. The most mechanical workflow in the BOOK family. Takes a polished manuscript and produces an automation-ready intermediate — a structured set of files and a BOOK_SPEC.json configuration that a mechanical pipeline (not AI) consumes to produce the final print-ready output (e.g., Lulu.com PDF). The AI's job is validation and metadata generation, NOT typesetting or PDF production. Triggered by the phrase 'BOOK_PRODUCTION'. Owner: Michael. Orchestrator: QUEEN.

on_engage: QUEEN reads this JSON in full
QUEEN reads BOOK_MANIFEST.json from project root (especially the production section)
QUEEN reads LULU_SPEC (or appropriate target spec) from infrastructure
QUEEN verifies polished chapter files exist in chapters/
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_PRODUCTION mode engaged. Target: [format]. Ready.'

relationship_to_book_family.downstream: LULU_PIPELINE or other mechanical automation (not a BOOK workflow — a build script)
relationship_to_book_family.contract: BOOK_PRODUCTION produces BOOK_SPEC.json + front matter + back matter + validated chapter files. The mechanical pipeline reads these and produces the final print-ready artifact.

required_files: BOOK_MANIFEST.json with production section filled (trim_size, color_interior, target_format)
LULU_SPEC or target format spec (infrastructure document)

design_rationale.ai_does_not_produce_pdfs: The AI validates, generates metadata, and produces supporting files (front/back matter). Mechanical typesetting (LaTeX, Typst, WeasyPrint, or similar) is a deterministic build step that does not benefit from AI judgment. Separation ensures reproducibility and keeps the AI focused on decisions that require intelligence.
design_rationale.book_spec_as_contract: BOOK_SPEC.json is the contract between the AI workflow and the mechanical pipeline. It fully specifies every physical parameter of the book. The mechanical pipeline reads the spec and produces output — no ambiguity, no judgment calls.
design_rationale.front_back_matter_generation: Front and back matter (title page, copyright, TOC, bibliography, index, etc.) are generated or validated by the AI because they require content-level decisions (what to include, how to order, what text to use). The mechanical pipeline only handles their typesetting.

QUEEN.description: QUEEN is Claude in the main conversation. Spawns FORMATTER, manages QA, executes verdicts.
responsibilities: verify prerequisites (polished chapters, manifest, target spec)
load target format spec (LULU_SPEC or other)

FORMATTER.description: Constructive worker. Validates the polished manuscript, generates front and back matter, and produces BOOK_SPEC.json — the complete physical specification for the mechanical pipeline.
produces: front/ directory with front matter files
back/ directory with back matter files
validation report (any issues found in manuscript files)

manuscript_validation: all chapters present and accounted for (match STRUCTURE.md)
no placeholder text remaining (no TODO, FIXME, TBD, [INSERT], etc.)
no incomplete sections (sections with headers but no content)
consistent heading levels across all chapters

front_matter_generation.generation_rule: generate files listed in BOOK_MANIFEST.json structure.front_matter. If a front matter file already exists in front/, validate it rather than regenerating.
front_matter_generation.title_page_content: title, subtitle (if any), author name — derived from BOOK_MANIFEST.json
front_matter_generation.copyright_page_content: copyright notice, year, author, rights statement, ISBN (if in manifest), edition info
front_matter_generation.toc_content: generated from STRUCTURE.md — chapter titles and section headings with placeholder page numbers (mechanical pipeline fills real page numbers)

back_matter_generation.generation_rule: generate files listed in BOOK_MANIFEST.json structure.back_matter. If a back matter file already exists in back/, validate it rather than regenerating.
back_matter_generation.bibliography_note: if bibliography declared, FORMATTER collects all citations from chapter files and produces a formatted bibliography. If no citation system is used, FORMATTER flags this as a finding.
back_matter_generation.index_note: index generation is a significant task. FORMATTER produces a preliminary index by extracting key terms from chapters. The human should review and refine.
back_matter_generation.glossary_note: if glossary declared, FORMATTER extracts defined terms from chapters and produces a glossary. Human review expected.

book_spec_generation.description: BOOK_SPEC.json fully specifies the physical book. All values must be valid against the target format spec (LULU_SPEC).
calculated_fields: spine_width: calculated from page count × paper thickness per page (from target spec)
gutter: derived from trim size (from target spec tables)
page_count_estimate: derived from word count + figure count + front/back matter pages
human_specified_fields: fonts: from production template or defaults per target spec

does_not_do: modify chapter prose (chapter content is immutable at this stage)
produce PDFs or any typeset output
make aesthetic decisions not specified by manifest or templates
typeset or paginate (that's the mechanical pipeline's job)

QA_PRODUCTION.description: Validates the FORMATTER's output against the target format spec and completeness requirements.
produces: production QA report: issues found, categorized

file_integrity: all files in BOOK_SPEC.json file_manifest actually exist
file ordering in manifest matches STRUCTURE.md
no extra files in chapters/front/back that aren't in the manifest
spec_compliance: trim_size is a supported size in target spec
margins within target spec bounds for declared trim size
spine_width correctly calculated (page_count × thickness, verify formula)
bleed values match target spec requirements
PDF standard declaration is valid for target
content_completeness: all front matter files declared in BOOK_MANIFEST exist and have content
all back matter files declared in BOOK_MANIFEST exist and have content
no placeholder text in any file (TODO, FIXME, TBD, [INSERT], etc.)
TOC entries match actual chapter/section structure
structural_consistency: chapter heading levels consistent across all files
figure/table/equation numbering sequential and correct (if applicable)

book_spec_schema.description: Schema for BOOK_SPEC.json — the contract between AI workflow and mechanical pipeline

fields.title: string
fields.subtitle: string or null
fields.author: string
fields.bleed_inches: number (per target spec, typically 0.125 for Lulu)
fields.spine_width_inches: number (calculated from page count × paper thickness)

cover.cover_file: string path or null (if cover handled separately)

fields.target_spec_version: string — version of LULU_SPEC or target spec used

actions: verify BOOK_MANIFEST.json exists with production section filled
verify polished chapter files exist in chapters/
verify STRUCTURE.md exists
load LULU_SPEC (or target format spec) from infrastructure
if target spec not found: report missing spec, ESCALATE
initialize INPROGRESS.md entry for BOOK_PRODUCTION engagement
QUEEN assembles context packet: all chapter files + STRUCTURE.md + BOOK_MANIFEST.json + LULU_SPEC + WORKER_FORMATTER.md + WORKER_PROTOCOL.md
QUEEN spawns FORMATTER
FORMATTER returns: BOOK_SPEC.json + front/ files + back/ files + validation report
QUEEN writes FORMATTER report to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN proceeds to QA
QUEEN spawns QA_PRODUCTION with: BOOK_SPEC.json + all files + STRUCTURE.md + BOOK_MANIFEST.json + LULU_SPEC
QA_PRODUCTION returns: QA report + verdict recommendation
QUEEN evaluates QA results → determines verdict
QUEEN writes FIX marker to INPROGRESS.md with QA findings
QUEEN re-spawns FORMATTER with: QA findings as correction directive + all prior context
FORMATTER addresses flagged issues (regenerate front/back matter, fix BOOK_SPEC values, etc.)
QUEEN re-spawns QA_PRODUCTION (full re-check)
fix_counter++

main_sequence: PRESTEP: verify prerequisites, load target spec
FORMATTING: FORMATTER validates manuscript, generates front/back matter, produces BOOK_SPEC.json
QA: QA_PRODUCTION validates output against target spec and completeness
if FIX: FORMATTER re-spawned → full QA re-run (loop)

actions: QUEEN writes completion summary to INPROGRESS.md
output files finalized: BOOK_SPEC.json, front/, back/, chapters/ (validated)
QUEEN commits all output to project root
QUEEN reports: 'BOOK_PRODUCTION complete. Automation-ready output in project root. Run LULU_PIPELINE (or target pipeline) to produce print-ready output.'

FIX.trigger: QA_PRODUCTION finds fixable issues (missing front matter, incorrect calculations, spec violations, formatting inconsistencies)

ESCALATE.trigger: fix_counter >= 3 OR QA finds issues requiring human judgment OR target spec missing OR manuscript has prose-level issues that should have been caught in BOOK_EDITORIAL
actions: QUEEN writes escalation summary to INPROGRESS.md
QUEEN pauses worker spawning
QUEEN reports to human: what's blocked, whether the issue is production-level or needs return to BOOK_EDITORIAL
QUEEN waits for human direction
ESCALATE.resumption: human resolves; QUEEN resumes or directs return to appropriate upstream workflow
ESCALATE.escalation_note: if QA finds prose-level issues (placeholder text, incomplete sections), this means BOOK_EDITORIAL missed them. ESCALATE with recommendation to return to BOOK_EDITORIAL rather than fixing prose in BOOK_PRODUCTION.

counter_semantics.fix_counter: increments on each FIX cycle. At 3, auto-escalate.

context_access.note: no cleanroom roles — all workers need full visibility

notes.mechanical_pipeline_note: BOOK_PRODUCTION's output feeds a mechanical pipeline (LULU_PIPELINE or equivalent). The mechanical pipeline is a build script — it reads BOOK_SPEC.json + file manifest and produces the final print-ready artifact. It is NOT an AI workflow. It is deterministic software.
notes.no_prose_modification_note: At this stage, chapter content is immutable. FORMATTER validates but does not modify prose. If prose issues are found, the correct action is ESCALATE with recommendation to return to BOOK_EDITORIAL.
notes.cover_note: Cover design/generation is outside the scope of BOOK_PRODUCTION. BOOK_SPEC.json has a cover section for specifying cover parameters, but cover file generation is a separate process. FORMATTER sets cover.cover_file to null if no cover file exists.
notes.lulu_spec_note: LULU_SPEC is an infrastructure document capturing Lulu.com's current print requirements. It must be researched and maintained separately. BOOK_PRODUCTION reads it but does not modify it. If LULU_SPEC is outdated or missing, QUEEN escalates.
notes.page_count_note: Page count is estimated from word count + figure count + front/back matter, using per-page word density from the target spec. This is an estimate — the mechanical pipeline calculates the actual page count during typesetting. Spine width is calculated from the estimated page count and may need adjustment after typesetting.
